_readme: MANUAL OVERRIDES. This layer pins hand-adjusted values so the mechanical scrape can never silently overwrite a known, deliberate correction. Keys are the FULL ARTICLE URL exactly as it appears on the site (the permalink, e.g. 'https://www.accc.gov.au/about-us/news/media-updates/some-article'). Any key beginning with an underscore is IGNORED (these notes and the example below). For each real URL key you may set any of: type ('Media Release' or 'Update'), category (string), title (string), dateISO ('YYYY-MM-DD' — the display date auto-updates to match unless you also set dateText), dateText (string, e.g. '10 January 2024'), notes (string, shown as a small 'manual' tag on that row). Pipeline order: scrape -> merge these on top, so the fields you set here always win. To use one, copy the _example shape to a top-level real URL key and fill in the values.

https://www.accc.gov.au/about-us/news/me.title: Corrected headline goes here
https://www.accc.gov.au/about-us/news/me.notes: Example only — this key sits under _example so it is ignored. Copy this shape to a real article URL at the top level to activate it.
